description: The Supreme Eiye Confraternity (SEC) is a Nigerian confraternity founded in 1965 at the University of Ibadan. Originally a student group, it has evolved into a criminal organization involved in drug trafficking, passport forgery, money laundering, and sex trafficking. It operates internationally with cells called 'forums' and is outlawed under Nigerian law.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 2014, Italian police concluded an investigation into the SEC. They found that the group, along with the Black Axe, had been active in Italy since at least 2008, and had established an organized criminal network with connections in other European countries which was directly involved in trafficking of women from rural Nigeria into Europe.
country_links.context: The SEC has been active in Italy since at least 2008, establishing an organized criminal network involved in trafficking women from Nigeria into Europe.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 2021, two Eiye members were arrested in Pretoria , South Africa in September 2021, in connection to online scams.
country_links.context: Two Eiye members were arrested in Pretoria, South Africa in connection to online scams, indicating the SEC's presence there.

country_links.evidence_quote: It is also reported to have operated a sex trafficking operation in Barcelona , Spain until a police raid in 2016, which resulted in 23 arrests.
country_links.context: The SEC operated a sex trafficking operation in Barcelona, Spain until a police raid in 2016.
